mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, directly compressing required returns and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. This mechanism operates continuously whenever rate cuts are observed, independent of other macro conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.83% as of Oct 2019 reflects ongoing easing cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines do not exceed baseline returns by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment signals robust labor market strength and consumer income growth, which sustains corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations. Employment growth above 100k per month triggers positive forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151945k as of Oct 2019 reflects sustained employment gains
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 100k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth reflects consumer spending momentum and aggregate demand strength. When RSAFS rises above its 6-month moving average, equity valuations expand due to improved earnings visibility, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction signals weakening economic capacity utilization and forward earnings pressure. When INDPRO declines more than 0.5% month-over-month, equity markets price in reduced corporate profitability, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate combined with declining payroll employment signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. This joint signal predicts negative 60-day forward returns as markets anticipate earnings compression and recession risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 2 months AND PAYEMS declines > 50k in same period
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% and PAYEMS at 151945k as of Oct 2019
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC < 0.015 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts contraction combined with declining retail sales signals broad-based demand weakness. When both HOUST and RSAFS fall below their 3-month moving averages simultaneously, equity markets reprice downward earnings expectations, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1256k and RSAFS at 526540M as of Oct 2019
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.018 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% annually combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary tightening pressure. In this regime, equity valuations compress as real discount rates rise, producing negative 5-day forward returns as markets react to tightening signals.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 257.27 and FEDFUNDS at 1.83% as of Oct 2019
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.012 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 combined with housing starts expansion signals robust economic expansion and forward earnings growth. When both indicators show quarter-over-quarter acceleration, equity valuations expand, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.0% AND HOUST > 1200k units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19112.54B and HOUST at 1256k as of Oct 2019
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.020 over any 3-year rolling window.
